title: Observation Date
notes: The calendar date of the observation. For raw (daily) data this is the trading date as returned by Yahoo Finance. For resampled datasets this is the period-end date (last trading day of the week/month/quarter/year). Expressed in YYYY-MM-DD format. Sourced from Yahoo Finance via the yfinance Python library using stock.history().

title: ISO Calendar Week Number
notes: The ISO 8601 calendar week number of the observation date, ranging from 1 to 53. Present only in weekly-frequency datasets (gig_data_weekly.csv, sp500_data_weekly.csv). Derived using pandas dt.isocalendar().week from the Date column inside save_resampled_data().

title: Calendar Month Number
notes: The calendar month of the observation date, ranging from 1 (January) to 12 (December). Present in weekly and monthly-frequency datasets. Derived using pandas dt.month from the Date column inside save_resampled_data().

title: Calendar Quarter Number
notes: The calendar quarter of the observation date, ranging from 1 (January-March) to 4 (October-December). Present in weekly, monthly, and quarterly-frequency datasets. Derived using pandas dt.quarter from the Date column inside save_resampled_data().

title: Calendar Year
notes: The four-digit calendar year of the observation date. Present in all resampled datasets. Derived using pandas dt.year from the Date column inside save_resampled_data().

title: Company Name
notes: The full display name of the publicly traded company (e.g. 'Uber', 'Upwork'). Used as a human-readable identifier alongside the Ticker symbol. For gig economy datasets this is the user-defined label from gig_tickers; for S&P 500 datasets this is sourced from the Wikipedia S&P 500 constituent table.

title: Stock Ticker Symbol
notes: The exchange ticker symbol identifying a publicly traded security (e.g. 'UBER', 'UPWK', 'AAPL'). For gig economy datasets the tickers are hand-curated; for S&P 500 datasets they are drawn from the Wikipedia S&P 500 constituent list. Used as the primary grouping key throughout all resampling and lag-feature operations.

title: GICS Industry Classification Key
notes: The GICS (Global Industry Classification Standard) industry-level classification key for the company as returned by Yahoo Finance's stock.info API (e.g. 'internet-content-information', 'software-application'). Provides a more granular grouping than sectorKey. May be null for companies where Yahoo Finance does not supply this field.

title: GICS Sector Classification Key
notes: The GICS (Global Industry Classification Standard) sector-level classification key for the company as returned by Yahoo Finance's stock.info API (e.g. 'technology', 'consumer-cyclical'). Represents a broader grouping than industryKey. May be null for companies where Yahoo Finance does not supply this field.

title: Closing Stock Price
notes: The adjusted closing price of the stock for the observation period, denominated in US dollars. For raw (daily) data this is the daily close. For resampled frequencies this is the last closing price within the period (week/month/quarter/year), using 'last' aggregation in save_resampled_data(). Sourced from Yahoo Finance via stock.history().

title: Trading Volume
notes: The number of shares traded during the observation period. For raw (daily) data this is the daily share volume. For resampled frequencies this is the last volume observation within the period, using 'last' aggregation in save_resampled_data(). Sourced from Yahoo Finance via stock.history().

title: Closing Stock Price (1-Period Lag)
notes: The closing stock price from the immediately prior period, shifted by one observation within each Ticker group. Enables period-over-period return calculations and autocorrelation analysis. The first observation for each Ticker will be NaN. Derived by applying groupby('Ticker')['Close'].shift(1) within save_resampled_data().

title: Trading Volume (1-Period Lag)
notes: The trading volume from the immediately prior period, shifted by one observation within each Ticker group. Supports volume momentum and autocorrelation analysis. The first observation for each Ticker will be NaN. Derived by applying groupby('Ticker')['Volume'].shift(1) within save_resampled_data().
